summary: Tested integration of student-status feature into /students page (Frontend only per request). Visual UI works correctly: page loads, status filter Picker shows all 5 options + 'كل الحالات', selection-mode button ('تحديد') activates the bulk-action bar which contains both 'تغيير الحالة' (purple) and 'تغيير المستوى' (purple) buttons side-by-side, search works, status filter narrows results (graduated=0 confirms previous iteration's cleanup persisted), no SideMenu link to /student-status-manager, no console/page errors. HOWEVER, the new data-testid selectors required by the spec are NOT reachable in the DOM (bulk-change-status-btn, bulk-change-level-btn, enter-selection-mode-btn, status-opt-{value}, status-new-level, status-reason, apply-status-btn). Root cause: these were written as JSX prop `data-testid=...` on React-Native TouchableOpacity / TextInput components, but react-native-web only forwards the `testID` prop to the underlying DOM as `data-testid`. The existing working selectors (e.g. the badge at line 840 `testID={`status-${item.id}`}`) confirm this. As a result, automated tests using the spec selectors will fail even though the UI behaviour is correct.

ui_bugs.issue: Button uses prop `data-testid` instead of `testID` — attribute not rendered to DOM in react-native-web
enter-selection-mode-btn not in DOM (data-testid vs testID)

status-opt-{value}, status-new-level, status-reason, apply-status-btn likely all use `data-testid` instead of `testID` (lines 1649, 1671, 1693, 1714). Not verified in DOM because modal could not be opened via spec selector — but same pattern in source code.

issues: شريط الفلاتر مرتب ونظيف؛ الـ status Picker الجديد يظهر بمحاذاة الأقسام والمستوى والشعبة بدون تكدّس.

action_items: In /app/frontend/app/students.tsx, rename every JSX prop `data-testid="..."` (on TouchableOpacity / TextInput / View) to `testID="..."`. Specifically lines: 1001, 1013, 1028, 1098 (bulk-change-level-btn), 1106 (bulk-change-status-btn), 1144 (enter-selection-mode-btn), 1607 (select-level-{level}), 1649 (status-opt-{value}), 1671 (status-new-level), 1693 (status-reason), 1714 (apply-status-btn), 1826, 1834. The badge already uses `testID` correctly (line 840) and resolves to DOM.
After the rename, re-test the apply-status-btn → POST /api/student-status/bulk-change flow end-to-end.
critical_code_review_comments: students.tsx is now ~2384 lines — clearly over the 700-line guideline. Consider splitting: filter bar component, bulk-actions bar, status modal, level modal, delete modal each into separate files under /app/frontend/src/components/students/.
STATUS_OPTIONS array is duplicated across pages; extract to /src/constants/studentStatus.ts and import in both students.tsx and any future consumer.
Mixing `data-testid` (HTML attribute) and `testID` (RN prop) inside the same RN component tree is the root cause of the missing selectors — adopt `testID` everywhere for consistency.

success_rate.frontend: UI behaviour 100%, test-selector contract 30% (status-{id} works; new bulk/modal selectors do not appear in DOM)

seed_data_creation: None. Previous iteration's status changes were cleaned up — confirmed: graduated filter returns 0 students.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent replaces `data-testid` with `testID` in students.tsx, run the same flow: login admin → /students → pick faculty/dept → click 'تحديد' → click student row → expect [data-testid=bulk-change-status-btn] visible → click → modal opens with status-opt-{value} buttons + status-new-level + status-reason + apply-status-btn. POST /api/student-status/bulk-change should succeed and badge status-{studentId} appears for non-active statuses. Status filter Picker (3rd <select> on the page) already filters correctly server-side.
rca of the issue: react-native-web maps RN's `testID` prop to the DOM attribute `data-testid`. JSX `data-testid` is treated as an unknown prop on RN components (TouchableOpacity, TextInput) and silently dropped — it does NOT reach the HTML output. The fix is purely mechanical: rename the prop to `testID`. The existing working selector `status-{item.id}` (line 840) uses `testID` correctly and is reachable in DOM.
